title: NOC 14100 – General Office Support Workers | GTR Immigration
description: General Office Support Workers play a crucial role in Canada’s private and public sector workplaces, supporting daily administrative operations across industries. Classified under NOC 14100, this occupation continues to experience strong post-pandemic hiring demand and offers immigration opportunities through work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and selected PR-linked pathways.

classification.teer: Consistent demand nationwideEntry-level TEER category with growth potentialEmployer-supported hiringCompetitive wagesOpportunities to progress into supervisory or specialized admin roles
classification.major_group: 141 – Office, court, and data support occupations
classification.minor_group: 1410 – Office support and court services occupations

common_job_titles: Administrative clerk Aircraft records clerk File and classification clerk Filing clerk General office worker Medical records clerk Office assistant Office clerk Records filing-system clerk Technical records clerk Ward clerk – hospital Warranty clerk Course coordinator
duties: Respond to telephone, electronic, or in-person inquiries
Assist with meeting agendas and minutes
Organize work flow for office staff
Retrieve and copy records upon request
Track documents removed from filing systems
employment_requirements: Business or commercial courses at school or college level are an advantage
Basic computer and filing system knowledge
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to generate 42,200 new positions for General Office Support Workers, while 35,300 job seekers are expected—creating favourable conditions for overseas applicants planning long-term settlement.
additional_information: General Office Support Workers are employed across:
Government officesHospitals and clinicsEducational institutionsCorporate officesLogistics firmsManufacturing companies
Why this occupation is attractive for immigrants:
Typical annual salaries range between $28,900 and $61,600 CAD, depending on province and experience.
Why Choose Canada in This Occupation?
Universal public healthcareStable employment environmentsStrong labour protectionsMulticultural communitiesClear PR transition strategiesFamily sponsorship options after PR approval
